title: Permission denied is returned when trying to access some host folders mounted in Hyper-V machines
body:
## Describe the problem

In Hyper-V machines, by default, the host `C:\` drive is mounted in `/mnt/c` and accessible from a container using a volume (`podman run -v C:\anysubfolder ...`).

However trying to access some folders such as `%HOME%` results in a permission denied error:

```console
> podman run -it --rm -v %HOME%:/jeff fedora:41 bash
[root@806658ac8ff1 /]# ls /jeff
ls: reading directory '/jeff/': Permission denied
```

## Other details

Accessing a subfolder of an `access denied` folder works fine:

```console
[root@806658ac8ff1 /]# ls /jeff/git
...no error...
```

On a WSL machine the `access denied` error isn't returned. WSL uses [drvfs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/wsl2-mount-disk) mounts, Hyper-V uses [v9fs](https://docs.kernel.org/filesystems/9p.html) (both use the [p9 protocol](https://ericvh.github.io/9p-rfc/) from [Plan9](https://9p.io/plan9/)).

This behavior is observable on a regualar v5.4.1 Podman installation on Windows. This issue is related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21036 (see [comment](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21036#issuecomment-2740203271))

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@lstocchi is planning to have a look at this issue

I have investigated this and found out the source of the error. I have documented my findings in https://github.com/hugelgupf/p9/issues/98 and https://github.com/hugelgupf/p9/issues/99.

In short, the permission error is shown because the home directory on Windows contains directory junctions that break the directory reading logic in the server library.
